result:
## Summary

The GSC export and the site's actual behavior told a clear story: **Lumina pivoted from a completely different product (InboxZap, an email-cleanup tool)**, and the pivot left real, traffic-killing bugs in the codebase. I fixed them directly and verified with a full production build + live route checks:

**Fixed:**
1. **`app/layout.tsx` had the old product's title/description/OG tags** ("InboxZap — Clean Your Inbox in Minutes") while every page's actual content was already "Lumina." Deploying this repo as-is would have regressed the live site's title tags back to the wrong brand.
2. **`/features` and `/pricing` didn't exist in the source at all** — no `app/features/` or `app/pricing/` directories — despite being linked from nav and the homepage's primary "Start free" CTA. Recreated both from the live content.
3. **The blog was fully broken**: client-side fetch to `/api/posts`, which 404s. Rebuilt it as real statically-generated pages (`app/blog/posts.ts` + `app/blog/[slug]/page.tsx`), with 5 new posts targeting actual Lumina buyer-intent topics (AI meeting assistants, action-item follow-through, Zoom/Meet/Teams notetaking, async recaps, better meeting notes).
4. **`/faq` redirected to a `#faq` anchor that didn't exist** on the homepage. Added a real FAQ section with `FAQPage` schema markup (also makes it eligible for rich results).
5. **Sitemap was a stub** (4 static URLs, TODO comment) — now includes all live blog posts.
6. **7 old blog URLs** (`/blog/post-6` through `post-12`) covered the pre-pivot product and were still indexed. Redirected them (301) to `/blog` instead of leaving them as 404s.
7. Added `metadataBase` — was missing, so OG/Twitter image URLs would've resolved to `localhost:3000` in production.

Verified via `npm run build` (clean, no warnings, all 13 routes generated) and by running the production server and curling every route.

**Strategic note:** all 39 GSC queries are legacy InboxZap terms (inbox zero, gmail storage, etc.) with terrible CTR/position — that traffic wouldn't convert to Lumina signups anyway, so I didn't try to rescue it. The new blog content targets Lumina's real category instead.

**What I couldn't do:** no `DISTRIBB_API_KEY` is set in this environment, so I couldn't pull real keyword volumes, run the backlink exchange, or connect GSC through Distribb itself — I worked from the raw CSV export and site crawl instead. To keep this going, sign up at distribb.io, connect the CMS + Google Search Console, and I can pick up keyword research, the backlink exchange, and ongoing `/optimize` passes from there.

One unrelated flag: `npm install` reported a **critical** security vulnerability in `next@14.2.5` — worth a dependency bump separately from this SEO work.
